blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ PPHN?
Incidence: 0.4–6.8/1,000 live births; mortality 7.6% (10.7% ใน severe cases); NDI ~25% ของ survivors. PPHN = failure to achieve/sustain normal ↓PVR at birth → right-to-left extrapulmonary shunting → hypoxemia. มี 3 categories: maladaptation (MAS, sepsis, RDS), excessive muscularization (chronic fetal hypoxia, idiopathic), underdevelopment (CDH, oligohydramnios). iNO (inhaled nitric oxide) = only FDA-approved specific pulmonary vasodilator (20 ppm); ลด ECMO need. Oxygenation Index (OI) = (MAP × FiO₂ × 100)/PaO₂; OI >25 → iNO; OI >40 → ECMO consideration.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย normal fetal pulmonary vascular transition: NO-cGMP, PGI₂-cAMP pathways
แยก 3 categories ของ PPHN: maladaptation / excessive muscularization / underdevelopment
รู้ maternal/prenatal/postnatal risk factors สำหรับ PPHN
คำนวณ Oxygenation Index และ interpret ค่า
รู้ echocardiographic diagnosis: pre/post-ductal SpO₂ gradient, TR jet, shunt direction
รู้ management principles: gentle ventilation, oxygen target SpO₂ 91–97%, pH >7.30 (ไม่ใช้ alkalosis)
รู้ iNO: mechanism, dose (20 ppm), response (40% fail), weaning, rebound PH
รู้ adjunct therapies: sildenafil (PDE5i), milrinone (LV dysfunction), bosentan (ET antagonist), treprostinil
รู้ CDH: LV hypoplasia, left-to-right atrial shunt pattern, caution with iNO
รู้ BPD-PH: incidence 16–25% in BPD, echo screening at 36 wk CGA
1. Normal Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Development
1.1 Fetal State
Only 10–20% combined ventricular output to lungs; rest via DA to descending aorta
High PVR maintained by: fluid-filled alveoli (mechanical), hypoxic vasoconstriction, ↑endothelin-1/thromboxane/leukotrienes, ↓NO/PGI₂
Late gestation: ↑PVR (when corrected for GA) despite growing vascular bed
Vasodilatory response to O₂ and acetylcholine emerges in late gestation
1.2 Transitional Changes at Birth
First breath + ↑O₂ → massive ↓PVR → >8–10× ↑pulmonary blood flow
NO produced by pulmonary endothelium → sGC → ↑cGMP → vasodilation; PDE5 degrades cGMP (peak activity at birth)
PGI₂ → adenylate cyclase → ↑cAMP → vasodilation; PDE3A degrades cAMP
↑Left atrial pressure → foramen ovale closes; ↑O₂ → ductus constricts (prostaglandin synthesis ↓)
Complete: 2 circulations in series; each ventricle carries equal output

Pathway Summary: NO-cGMP and PGI₂-cAMP
NO pathway: eNOS → NO → sGC → ↑cGMP → vasodilation; PDE5 degrades cGMP. Sildenafil inhibits PDE5 → ↑cGMP → prolongs vasodilation. PGI₂ pathway: PGI₂ → adenylate cyclase → ↑cAMP → vasodilation; PDE3A degrades cAMP. Milrinone inhibits PDE3 → ↑cAMP → inodilator effect. Both pathways can be therapeutic targets in PPHN.
2. Pathophysiology and Classification
2.1 PPHN Definition
Failure to achieve/sustain normal ↓PVR → sustained ↑PVR → R→L extrapulmonary shunting across DA/PFO → hypoxemia
"Persistent fetal circulation" = original term (Gersony 1969)
2.2 Three Categories
2.3 Abnormal Vasoregulation
↓eNOS expression → ↓NO; ↑PDE5 activity → ↓cGMP
↑Endothelin-1 (potent vasoconstrictor); ETA receptors (vasoconstriction) > ETB receptors (vasodilation) in PPHN
Oxidant stress → ↓sGC activity, ↑PDE5 → limits iNO effect
Hyperoxia independently ↑PDE5 activity (Farrow KN Circ Res 2008) → partially explains iNO non-response
3. Etiology and Risk Factors
3.1 Prenatal Risk Factors
Maternal: Black/Asian race, BMI >27, diabetes, asthma → ↑PPHN risk
NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen) in 3rd trimester → premature DA constriction → vascular remodeling; aspirin = consistent risk factor

SSRIs in 2nd half of pregnancy: population studies show ↑PPHN incidence (but effect size modest, some studies negative); maternal depression itself = adverse pregnancy outcome risk → primary consideration = maternal well-being
C-section delivery → delays ↓PAP; delivery <39 wk amplifies this
LGA infant, male sex
3.2 Postnatal Risk Factors
Perinatal asphyxia: acute → reversible vasoconstriction; chronic in utero → vascular remodeling
Therapeutic hypothermia (33.5°C): pooled RCT analysis showed no ↑PPHN incidence; deep hypothermia (30–32°C) problematic in animals
Trisomy 21: 10-fold ↑idiopathic PPHN; 5.2% Dutch cohort; overrepresented in ECMO patients
Down syndrome → AV canal → pulmonary HTN from ↑flow (different mechanism)
3.3 Genetic/Rare Causes
Alveolar capillary dysplasia (ACD): FOXF1 mutation/deletion (~40% cases); universally lethal; lung biopsy required; >50% have other anomalies
Surfactant deficiency: SP-B, SP-C, ABCA3 genes
TTF-1/Nkx2.1 disruption; TBX4 mutation
Carbamoylphosphate synthase polymorphism (↓NO substrate)
Inborn errors of metabolism: methylmalonic acidemia
4. Clinical Evaluation and Diagnosis
4.1 Clinical Presentation
Cyanosis within hours of birth; poorly responsive to O₂ supplementation
Labile hypoxemia (rapid swings with minimal stimulation)
Pre-ductal (right hand) SpO₂ > post-ductal (foot) SpO₂: gradient >5–10% → right-to-left DA shunting
Reversed differential cyanosis (lower > upper SpO₂) → TGA with PPHN (unusual)
Loud single S2; systolic murmur of TR
4.2 Oxygenation Index
OI = (MAP × FiO₂ × 100) / PaO₂
OI 15–22: moderate PPHN; consider surfactant if parenchymal disease
OI >25: iNO indication (Class I, Level A — AHA/ATS 2015)
OI >40: ECMO consideration/transfer
4.3 Echocardiography — Gold Standard
Rules out structural CHD (coarctation, TAPVR, TGA)
Confirms PPHN: bidirectional or predominantly R→L shunting at PFO/PDA
Assesses LV and RV function
TAPSE + RV GLPS + predominantly R→L PDA shunt → best predictors of progression to death/ECMO (Malowitz 2015)
CDH pattern: R→L at DA + L→R at atria → LV dysfunction; limits iNO response in first few days
Radiographic and lab findings by PPHN type:
5. Management Principles
5.1 General Principles (AHA/ATS 2015)
Maintain: normal temperature (unless hypothermia Rx), electrolytes (esp. Ca²⁺), glucose, Hgb, intravascular volume
"Gentle ventilation": avoid paralysis; PaO₂ 50–70 mmHg, PaCO₂ 40–60 mmHg; NO hyperventilation (alkalosis not recommended)
pH >7.30 (acidosis = pulmonary vasoconstrictor); alkalosis (pH >7.45) → paradoxically ↑PVR + ↑permeability + ↓CBF → AVOID
Optimal O₂: preductal SpO₂ 91–97% (Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2009); avoid hyperoxia (↑PDE5, ↑ROS)
Surfactant: only with parenchymal disease; ↓ECMO in MAS (meta-analysis); NOT useful in idiopathic PPHN
Systemic hemodynamics: dopamine 5–20 mcg/kg/min first-line; milrinone 0.2–0.99 mcg/kg/min (inodilator, esp. LV dysfunction); vasopressin (V1 + eNOS activation) for severe PPHN
5.2 Lung Recruitment
When parenchymal disease present: optimize PEEP (8–9 posterior ribs on AP CXR = adequate FRC)
High-frequency ventilation + iNO more effective than either alone (Kinsella J Pediatr 1997)
Under-inflation ↑intrapulmonary R→L shunting; over-inflation ↑PVR (mechanical compression) + impedes venous return
6. Inhaled Nitric Oxide (iNO)
6.1 Mechanism and Dosing
NO diffuses from alveolus → smooth muscle → sGC → ↑cGMP → selective pulmonary vasodilation
Inactivated by hemoglobin → minimal systemic effect (selective!)
Standard dose: 20 ppm; higher doses (>80 ppm) → methemoglobinemia; no additional benefit above 20 ppm
Methemoglobin risk: very low at <40 ppm; mainly with FiO₂ >60% simultaneously
Rebound PH: when iNO discontinued rapidly → wean slowly; sildenafil may help during weaning
6.2 Response and Non-Response
40% of infants do NOT respond or sustain response to iNO
Most common reason: inadequate lung recruitment → optimize PEEP first
True non-responders: analyze parenchymal disease + vascular disease + cardiac dysfunction
CDH: LV dysfunction → poor iNO response (PGE1/milrinone may improve LV → better iNO response)
Evaluate for ACD, surfactant deficiency, TAPVR if no improvement despite ECMO
6.3 Evidence
Clark et al. NEJM 2000;342:469: Low-dose iNO RCT. 20 ppm; ↓ECMO need. 1-yr follow-up: NDI not reduced vs control
Konduri GG et al. Pediatrics 2004: Randomized trial early vs standard iNO use. Early (OI ~20) did NOT reduce ECMO/death
NINOS 2000: OI 44 at enrollment. NDI 30% (control) vs 35% (iNO) — no difference; iNO does NOT improve neurodevelopment
Kinsella JP et al. J Pediatr 1997: iNO + HFV more effective than either alone for severe PPHN
7. Adjunct and Rescue Therapies
Drug reference table for PPHN management:

rows: Methemoglobin; rebound PH on rapid DC
IV: 0.4 mg/kg load over 3h; maint 1.6 mg/kg/day
IV: load 0.75 mcg/kg/min × 3h (omit if hypotension); maint 0.2–0.99 mcg/kg/min
Non-selective vasodilation; rebound PH on DC

blocks.text: 7.1 CDH Special Considerations
Complex PPHN: LV hypoplasia → LV dysfunction → pulmonary venous HTN
Pattern: R→L at DA + L→R at PFO (LA pressure elevated) → misleading
iNO caution: Class IIa with confirmed or suspected LV dysfunction (AHA/ATS 2015)
Milrinone: preferred to ↓LV afterload, ↑LV performance → then reassess iNO
"Maintain PDA patent" subset: severe LV dysfunction → PGE1 to maintain DA as "pop-off" valve
CDH-PH can persist for months/years; cardiac cath for chronic PH cases
7.2 BPD-Associated PH
Incidence 16–25% in BPD; ↑severe morbidity and mortality (~50% late death with severe BPD-PH)
Pathogenesis: impaired angiogenesis, vascular pruning, ↓alveolar-capillary surface
Screening: echo at 36 wk corrected GA in infants requiring O₂ or with recurrent cyanotic spells
Severe cases: cardiac catheterization for vasoreactivity testing
Treatment: sildenafil (oral) and/or bosentan; optimize lung disease
8. ECMO and Outcomes
8.1 ECMO
VV-ECMO: respiratory; VA-ECMO: cardiac support; OI >40 = transfer to ECMO center
>33,400 neonates treated with ECMO for respiratory failure; cumulative survival to DC 73% (ELSO 2021)
Neonatal ECMO rates ↓dramatically (80% in 1990 → ~8% in 2017) → improved medical management
UK ECMO Trial: 1-yr survival 68% (ECMO) vs 41% (conventional); similar disability rates at 7 yr
ECMO survivors: risk academic problems → detailed follow-up to school age
8.2 Neurodevelopmental Outcomes
Overall: NDI 14–30%; iNO does NOT reduce NDI; brain injury likely precedes ECMO
9. NICU Practical — PPHN ที่ NICU
ทารกแรกเกิด cyanosis ไม่ตอบ O₂ → คิด PPHN หรือ cyanotic CHD ก่อน
ทำ pre/post-ductal SpO₂ ทันที (right hand + foot); gradient >5–10% → PPHN until proven otherwise
OI คำนวณ: (MAP × FiO₂ × 100)/PaO₂; ตรวจทุก 4–6 hr; OI >25 → iNO; OI >40 → prepare ECMO
Echo ก่อน iNO เสมอ: rule out structural CHD (TAPVR obstructed, coarctation), assess LV function
เริ่ม iNO 20 ppm; ตรวจ methemoglobin ที่ 4 hr; ถ้าไม่ตอบสนองใน 30–60 min → optimize lung recruitment (PEEP)
Wean iNO ช้าๆ (ลด 5 ppm ทุก 4–6 hr); ถ้า rebound PH → พิจารณา oral sildenafil overlap
CDH: ระวัง iNO ถ้า LA pressure สูง (L→R PFO) + R→L DA → LV dysfunction pattern → milrinone ก่อน
BPD-PH: echo screen ที่ 36 wk CGA; ถ้า confirmed → sildenafil PO ± bosentan; optimize respiratory support
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Pre-ductal ≠ Post-ductal: Measure BOTH
ใส่ probe ที่ right hand (pre-ductal) + foot (post-ductal); gradient >5–10% → R→L PDA shunting; reversed differential cyanosis (lower > upper) → TGA + PPHN; ต้องวัด SIMULTANEOUSLY — ไม่ใช่คนละเวลา

PEARL 2 — Hyperoxia Worsens PPHN via PDE5 Upregulation
FiO₂ >60% → ↑PDE5 activity (Farrow 2008) → ↑cGMP degradation → limits iNO effect; target preductal SpO₂ 91–97%; อย่า "flood" O₂ เป็น routine — ใช้เหมือน drug ที่มี dose-response และ side effects

PEARL 3 — Alkalosis (pH >7.45) → Paradoxically Worsens PPHN
Historical practice: hyperventilate + sodium bicarb → alkalosis → "pulmonary vasodilation"; แต่ effect transient; prolonged alkalosis → ↑permeability, ↑PVR, ↓CBF → avoid; ปัจจุบัน: maintain pH 7.30–7.45, PaCO₂ 40–60 mmHg ("gentle ventilation")

PEARL 4 — 40% of PPHN Fail iNO → Check Lung Recruitment First
Most common reason for non-response: under-recruited lung → ↑intrapulmonary shunting; optimize PEEP (8–9 posterior ribs on AP CXR); HFV + iNO more effective than either alone; if still no response → think structural (ACD, TAPVR) or cardiac (LV dysfunction in CDH)

PEARL 5 — CDH Pattern Recognition: L→R PFO + R→L DA = LV Dysfunction
Pattern ใน CDH with severe PPHN: R→L shunting at DA (↑RV pressure) BUT L→R shunting at PFO (↑LA pressure from LV dysfunction → ↑pulmonary venous pressure); อย่าเพิ่ม iNO ใน pattern นี้ → จะ ↑pulmonary blood flow → worsen pulmonary venous HTN; ใช้ milrinone เพื่อ ↓LV afterload ก่อน
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Clark RH et al. NEJM. 2000;342:469 (Clinical Inhaled Nitric Oxide Research Group): Low-dose iNO RCT; 20 ppm ↓ECMO need vs placebo. NDI at 1 yr: no difference.
Konduri GG et al. Pediatrics. 2004;113:559: Early vs standard iNO (OI ~20 vs 25–40) RCT. Early iNO did NOT reduce ECMO/death (16.7% vs 19.5%).
Kinsella JP et al. J Pediatr. 1997;131:55: HFV + iNO vs either alone for severe PPHN. Combination superior; validates combined lung recruitment + vasodilation strategy.
Abman SH et al. Circulation. 2015;132:2037 (AHA/ATS Guidelines): Pediatric PH guidelines including PPHN management; evidence-based grading for iNO, ECMO, sildenafil, milrinone.
Lakshminrusimha S et al. Pediatr Res. 2009;66:539: Oxygen concentration and pulmonary hemodynamics in newborn lambs with PH. Lowest PVR at SpO₂ 91–97%; hyperoxia not beneficial.
Farrow KN et al. Circ Res. 2008;102:226: Hyperoxia increases PDE5 activity in ovine fetal pulmonary artery smooth muscle — mechanism for O₂-induced iNO resistance.
Neonatal Inhaled Nitric Oxide Study Group. J Pediatrics. 2000;136:611: NDI follow-up iNO trials. NDI ~25–35% overall; NOT reduced by iNO; timing of brain injury precedes ECMO.
Steinhorn RH et al. J Pediatr. 2009;155:841: IV sildenafil improved oxygenation in neonates with and without iNO. Useful for weaning iNO; rebound PH prevention.
Pierce CM et al. J Pediatr. 2021;237:154: IV sildenafil RCT (multicenter): did NOT reduce treatment failure or time on iNO vs placebo in PPHN.
Mourani PM et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2015;191:87: Early PVD in preterm at risk for BPD. Pulmonary HTN signs at 7 days predict late BPD-PH. Warrants early serial echo.
UK ECMO Trial Collaboration. Pediatrics. 1998;101:E1: Follow-up at 1, 4, 7 yr. Survival benefit ECMO maintained at 1–4 yr; disability rates similar at 7 yr. Brain injury intrinsic to disease.
Rosenzweig EB et al. Eur Respir J. 2019;53:1801916 (WSPH 2019): Updated PH classification; PPHN = Group 1.7 (separate subcategory PAH); BPD/CDH = Group 3 developmental lung diseases.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: OI คืออะไร และ threshold สำคัญในการบริหาร PPHN?

ตอบ: OI = (MAP × FiO₂ × 100)/PaO₂. OI >25 = iNO indication (AHA/ATS Class I-A); OI >40 = ECMO consideration/transfer. OI ต่ำกว่า 25 ใน RCT early iNO: ไม่ได้ลด ECMO/death → ไม่แนะนำเริ่ม iNO ก่อน OI ถึง threshold
Q: อธิบาย mechanism ของ iNO และทำไมจึง "selective" pulmonary vasodilator?

ตอบ: iNO diffuses from alveolus → vascular smooth muscle → activates sGC → ↑cGMP → vasodilation; เมื่อ NO เข้าสู่ bloodstream → จับ hemoglobin ทันที → inactivated → ไม่มี systemic vasodilation; เพราะ NO delivered to ventilated lung segments เท่านั้น → ยัง improve V/Q matching (dilates vessels next to ventilated alveoli)
Q: ทำไม 40% ของ PPHN ไม่ตอบสนอง iNO? และ step ต่อไปควรทำอะไร?

ตอบ: Most common: inadequate lung recruitment → ↑intrapulmonary R→L shunting from atelectasis → optimize PEEP; consider HFV; Hyperoxia → ↑PDE5 → ↓cGMP → ↓iNO effect; CDH: LV dysfunction → ↓iNO response → use milrinone first; True non-responders: evaluate structural (ACD, TAPVR, cardiac anatomy) → genetic testing; next drug options: sildenafil (PDE5i), inhaled prostacyclin, milrinone; ECMO if refractory
Q: CDH ทำให้ cardiac management ซับซ้อนอย่างไร?

ตอบ: LV hypoplasia in CDH → LV diastolic dysfunction → ↑LA pressure → pulmonary venous HTN; Echo pattern: R→L DA (elevated RV pressure) + L→R PFO (elevated LA pressure) → paradox; iNO in this setting → ↑pulmonary flow → worsen pulmonary venous HTN; Management: PGE1 to maintain DA as "safety valve" for RV; milrinone to ↓LV afterload, ↑LV performance → then consider iNO; some patients benefit from "permissive" R→L ductal shunting
Q: NSAIDs ในแม่มีผลต่อ PPHN อย่างไร?

ตอบ: NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen) in 3rd trimester → inhibit PG synthesis → premature DA constriction in utero → pressure overload → vascular remodeling (↑smooth muscle, ↑muscularization) → PPHN after birth; aspirin = consistent risk; selective COX-2 inhibitors: risk unclear; SSRIs: population studies show ↑PPHN incidence but effect small and not consistent; clinical implication: avoid NSAIDs in 3rd trimester if possible
Q: BPD-associated PH — when to screen and how to treat?

ตอบ: Screen: echo at 36 wk corrected GA for any infant still requiring O₂ or with recurrent cyanotic spells/CO₂ retention; echo signs: ventricular septal flattening, RV dilation, TR velocity; severe cases: cardiac cath (vasoreactivity testing); treatment: optimize lung disease first; sildenafil (oral) ↓PVR; bosentan (ETA/ETB antagonist) for chronic PH; treprostinil for severe/refractory; target SpO₂ >95% chronically in BPD-PH
Q: อธิบาย Alveolar Capillary Dysplasia (ACD) — ทำอย่างไรเมื่อสงสัย?

ตอบ: ACD = FOXF1 mutation/deletion (~40% cases) → simplification of lung architecture, paucity of capillaries, misaligned pulmonary veins (→ R→L intrapulmonary shunts), universally lethal; presentation: cyanosis + minimal parenchymal disease + refractory to ALL therapy including ECMO; >50% have other anomalies; diagnosis: lung biopsy or autopsy; วิธีสงสัย: PPHN with no parenchymal disease + no response to iNO + ECMO + clinical deterioration → lung biopsy; genetic testing for FOXF1
Q: Rebound PH เมื่อหยุด iNO — mechanism และวิธีป้องกัน?

ตอบ: Mechanism: iNO → ↑cGMP → ↓endogenous NO production (feedback inhibition of eNOS); abrupt DC → rapid ↓cGMP → reactive pulmonary vasoconstriction; prevention: (1) wean iNO slowly (5 ppm decrements q4–6h); (2) overlap with oral sildenafil before DC (PDE5 inhibitor → prolong cGMP); (3) stabilize hemodynamics before each dose reduction; (4) if rebound occurs → restart iNO at prior dose → wean more slowly
Day 48 Preview

Avery Ch.48 — Patent Ductus Arteriosus in the Preterm Infant (Clyman)
พรุ่งนี้: Patent Ductus Arteriosus in the Preterm Infant (Avery Ch.48, Clyman) — ต่อเนื่องจากสัปดาห์นี้ที่เรียนเรื่อง cardiovascular physiology; เราจะมาดู ductus arteriosus โดยเฉพาะ: ทำไม preterm PDA ไม่ปิด (PGE₂ sensitivity สูง, Kv channel ต่ำ), clinical vs echo diagnosis, hemodynamic effects (pulmonary overcirculation + systemic steal), และ treatment controversy (indomethacin vs ibuprofen vs acetaminophen vs conservative). Key question: to treat or not to treat the PDA?
